name: oauth2-flow-with-drive-api
description: Validate that the skill produces a secure OAuth2 implementation with PKCE for a web app accessing the Google Drive API, including token storage, refresh handling, and proper scope configuration.
input: Implement OAuth2 authentication with PKCE for a Next.js web application that needs to access users' Google Drive files. Users should be able to sign in with their Google account, browse their Drive files, and download selected files. Use the narrowest possible scopes. Store refresh tokens encrypted in a PostgreSQL database. Handle token refresh transparently. Implement proper error handling for revoked tokens and expired sessions.

assertions.description: Must request the narrowest scope (drive.readonly or drive.file) instead of the full drive scope to minimize security surface and simplify consent review.
Must implement PKCE with code_verifier and code_challenge parameters to protect the authorization code exchange from interception attacks.
Must handle refresh token storage and usage for maintaining long-lived access without requiring users to re-authorize on every session.
Must encrypt refresh tokens before storing in the database to protect user credentials from database compromise.
Must use the Drive API files.list endpoint with fields parameter to retrieve file metadata efficiently without over-fetching.

name: sheets-api-batch-operations
description: Validate that the skill produces efficient Sheets API batch operations with proper value handling, formula preservation, error handling, and quota management.
input: Build a reporting service that reads sales data from one Google Sheet, processes it, and writes summary reports to another Sheet. The source sheet has 10,000 rows with formulas in calculated columns. Read all data preserving formula results. Process the data to generate monthly summaries. Write the summaries to a target sheet using batch updates. Preserve existing formatting in the target sheet. Handle quota limits with batch requests. Use a service account for authentication.

assertions.description: Must set valueRenderOption (FORMATTED_VALUE or UNFORMATTED_VALUE) explicitly when reading to control whether formula results or raw values are returned.
Must use spreadsheets.values.batchUpdate or spreadsheets.batchUpdate for writing multiple ranges in a single API call to reduce quota consumption.
Must set valueInputOption (RAW or USER_ENTERED) explicitly when writing to control whether values are treated as literals or parsed as formulas.
Must authenticate using service account credentials for server-to-server access without user interaction.
Must request appropriate scopes (spreadsheets.readonly for reading, spreadsheets for writing) to follow the principle of least privilege.
